title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
summary: Jack Roberts discusses Claude Fable 5 being delisted and argues that users should deploy models locally to avoid vendor lock-in. He demonstrates running models via Ollama and building a NotebookLM alternative with open notebook software, advocating for local hosting as a resilience strategy.
writeup: The video responds to the withdrawal of Claude Fable 5 by making the case for running AI models locally rather than depending on cloud providers. Jack Roberts walks through using Ollama to run open-source models on personal hardware and building a local alternative to NotebookLM. The video also covers setting up model routing across multiple providers. The throughline is privacy-preserving inference and reducing reliance on any single vendor.
